records.ety: [OE. wis, AS. wīs; akin to OS. & OFries. wīs, D. wijs, G. weise, OHG. wīs, wīsi, Icel. vīss, Sw. vis, Dan. viis, Goth. weis; akin to wit, v. i. See Wit, v., and cf. Righteous, Wisdom.]

defs: Having knowledge; knowing; enlightened; of extensive information; erudite; learned.
quotes: They are wise to do evil, but to do good they have no knowledge.

defs: Hence, especially, making due use of knowledge; discerning and judging soundly concerning what is true or false, proper or improper; choosing the best ends and the best means for accomplishing them; sagacious.
quotes: When clouds appear, wise men put their cloaks.
From a child thou hast known the holy scriptures, which are able to make thee wise unto salvation.

defs: Versed in art or science; skillful; dexterous; specifically, skilled in divination.
quotes: Fal. There was, mine host, an old fat woman even now with me; but she's gone. Sim. Pray you, sir, was't not the wise woman of Brentford?

defs: Hence, prudent; calculating; shrewd; wary; subtle; crafty.
quotes: Nor, on the other side, Will I be penuriously wise As to make money, that's my slave, my idol.
Lords do not care for me: I am too wise to die yet.

defs: Dictated or guided by wisdom; containing or exhibiting wisdom; well adapted to produce good effects; judicious; discreet; as, a wise saying; a wise scheme or plan; wise conduct or management; a wise determination.
quotes: A very grave, state bachelor, my dainty one; He's wise in years, and of a temperate warmth.
You are too wise in years, too full of counsel, For my green experience.

records.ety: [OE. wise, AS. wīse; akin to OS. wīsa, OFries. wīs, D. wijs, wijze, OHG. wīsa, G. weise, Sw. vis, Dan. viis, Icel. öðruvīs otherwise; from the root of E. wit; hence, originally, knowledge, skill. See Wit, v., and cf. Guise.]

defs: Way of being or acting; manner; mode; fashion.
quotes: To love her in my beste wyse.
This song she sings in most commanding wise.
Let not these blessings then, sent from above, Abused be, or spilt in profane wise.
notes: ☞ This word is nearly obsolete, except in such phrases as in any wise, in no wise, on this wise, etc. “ Fret not thyself in any wise to do evil.” Ps. xxxvii. 8. “He shall in no wise lose his reward.” Matt. x. 42. “ On this wise ye shall bless the children of Israel.” Num. vi. 23.
☞ Wise is often used as a suffix in composition, as in likewise, nowise, lengthwise, etc., in which words -ways is often substituted with the same sense; as, noways, lengthways, etc.
